detailed_analysis: antigravity_io.py is a small (148-line), well-structured Layer-3 interface adapter implementing the CustomizationTypeIO parse/render contract for Antigravity SKILL.md skills. It is tool-agnostic in spirit: it delegates all YAML/frontmatter primitives to markdown_yaml_metadata_block, holds no hardcoded secrets or paths, and respects the Dependency Rule (imports only inward: canonical, markdown_yaml_metadata_block). Both public functions are under the 40-line limit (parse: 44 source lines incl. docstring, ~30 code lines; render: ~21 code lines) and cyclomatic complexity is low. The code is largely clean. The notable defects are correctness/robustness fail-fast gaps in render(), which reaches into canonical['name'] and canonical['pair_id'] with direct subscription (KeyError on a malformed canonical) while treating 'description' defensively with .get() — an inconsistency that contradicts the project's 'validate at boundaries, fail loud with a descriptive message' rule (NFR-14, AGENTS.md §8). There is also an asymmetry between parse and render: parse only writes canonical['name']/['description'] when present in the input frontmatter, but render unconditionally requires canonical['name'] — a canonical produced by a sibling tool's projection that omits 'name' would crash render with a bare KeyError rather than a structured error. No security smells, no global mutable state, no injection vectors. Spec conformance is strong: the file matches architecture §4 (Layer-3 adapter, ~varies lines), §3 Dependency Rule, §7 port shape, US-10 AC-1/AC-4. NFR-15 (secret_policy) is not applicable here because Antigravity supports only the 'skill' customization_type, which carries no env/headers/auth.* fields; the egress secret check lives in the mcp_server_io layer.

findings.evidence:
frontmatter["pair_id"] = canonical["pair_id"]
frontmatter["name"] = canonical["name"]
if canonical.get("description"):
    frontmatter["description"] = canonical["description"]
findings.problem: render() subscripts canonical["pair_id"] (line 120) and canonical["name"] (line 121) directly while treating description defensively with .get() (line 122). A canonical dict missing 'name' or 'pair_id' raises a bare, contextless KeyError at the egress boundary rather than the descriptive structured error AGENTS.md §8 / NFR-14 require. There is no assertion or boundary check that the canonical satisfies the renderer's required-field invariant (pair_id + name). The asymmetry (defensive on description, fail-hard on name/pair_id) is also a POLA/consistency smell.

findings.evidence:
parse: if "name" in frontmatter_data: canonical["name"] = str(...)   # only set when present
render: frontmatter["name"] = canonical["name"]                     # unconditionally required
findings.problem: parse() only writes canonical['name'] when the source frontmatter contains it (line 70-71), so a parsed Antigravity skill that omits the required 'name' field yields a canonical with no 'name' key. render() then unconditionally requires canonical['name'] (line 121). The two halves disagree on whether 'name' is mandatory: SKILL.md spec lists name+description as Required (module docstring lines 4-5), but parse does not enforce that requirement at the ingest boundary, deferring a hard failure to render time on a different artifact. This weakens round-trip-stability guarantees (US-10 AC contract item 1) for malformed-but-parseable input and contradicts fail-fast-at-boundary (NFR-14).

Chained .get(...).get(...) navigation of the nested passthrough bags. Depth is only 2 (below the >3 Demeter threshold) so this is a stylistic SMELL, not a violation: it silently substitutes {} for a structurally-absent bag. Given the file otherwise validates nothing about canonical shape, the silent default masks the case where canonical['per_agentic_tool_only'] exists but is not a dict — a malformed canonical would not fail loud here either. Low severity; flagged for consistency with the fail-fast finding above.

spec_adherence.notes: NFR-15 (secret_policy at egress) is not applicable: Antigravity supports only the 'skill' customization_type, which has no env/headers/auth.* fields; the secret check is correctly located in mcp_server_io. Architecture §3 Dependency Rule honoured (imports only canonical + markdown_yaml_metadata_block). §7 port shape honoured. US-10 AC-1 (parse/render/extract triple) and AC-4 (tool name 'antigravity' verbatim in per_agentic_tool_only/extra bags) honoured. US-10 AC contract item 4 (unknown fields preserved in per_agentic_tool_extra) honoured (lines 83-89, 130-132). The only spec-adjacent weaknesses are the fail-fast gaps under NFR-14, recorded as deviations below.

deviations.description: render_antigravity_skill_md (lines 120-121) does not validate that the canonical carries its required 'name' and 'pair_id' fields before subscripting them; a malformed canonical produces a bare KeyError, not the explicit boundary failure Clean Code / fail-fast (AGENTS.md §8) prescribes. parse (lines 70-73) likewise omits enforcing that the SKILL.md required fields name+description are present on ingest, despite the module docstring (lines 4-5) declaring them Required.

verdict_summary: Clean, well-isolated Layer-3 adapter; only minor fail-fast/boundary-validation gaps in render() and a parse/render required-field asymmetry. No CRITICAL/MAJOR findings.
